edges.description: Glycosidase deficiency leads to lysosomal accumulation of undegraded glycan substrates.
edges.source: Lysosomal Glycosidase Deficiency and Impaired Glycoprotein Catabolism

edges.description: Lysosomal storage drives CNS glial activation and neuronal loss.

nodes.description: Deficiency of a lysosomal glycosidase (e.g., alpha-L-fucosidase, alpha-mannosidase, aspartylglucosaminidase, sialidase) blocks the stepwise lysosomal degradation of N- and O-linked glycans of glycoproteins, glycolipids, and oligosaccharides.
nodes.id: Lysosomal Glycosidase Deficiency and Impaired Glycoprotein Catabolism

evidence.reference_title: Fucosidosis: A Review of a Rare Disease.
evidence.snippet: This enzyme is responsible for breaking down fucose-containing glycoproteins, glycolipids, and oligosaccharides within the lysosome

nodes.description: Undegraded oligosaccharides and glycopeptides accumulate in lysosomes, which become engorged with substrate, with secondary metabolic and downstream cellular consequences.

evidence.reference_title: Fucosidosis: A Review of a Rare Disease.
evidence.snippet: leading to the accumulation of fucose-rich substrates in lysosomes. Lysosomes become engorged with undigested substrates

nodes.description: CNS lysosomal dysfunction causes microglial activation, neuroinflammation, and neuronal loss, producing the progressive neurodegenerative features of the oligosaccharidoses.

evidence.reference_title: Fucosidosis: A Review of a Rare Disease.
evidence.snippet: The central nervous system is particularly vulnerable, with lysosomal dysfunction causing microglial activation, inflammation, and neuronal loss
